(born 29 October 1985) is an English film and TV actress. She first gained attention for her role as Ames in the second season of the television series *[Human Target](/wiki/Human_Target_(2010_TV_series) "Human Target (2010 TV series)")* (2010–11), and also for her appearance in the 2009 [straight-to-DVD](/wiki/Straight-to-DVD "Straight-to-DVD") film *[The Hills Run Red](/wiki/The_Hills_Run_Red_(2009_film) "The Hills Run Red (2009 film)")*. She played the lead character in the television drama *[Made in Jersey](/wiki/Made_in_Jersey "Made in Jersey")* (2012), cancelled after two episodes aired. From 2014 to 2017, she starred as the lead character, Mary Sibley, in the series *[Salem](/wiki/Salem_(TV_series) "Salem (TV series)")*. From 2018 to 2023, Montgomery played the role of Lauren Bloom on the [medical drama](/wiki/Medical_drama "Medical drama") television series *[New Amsterdam](/wiki/New_Amsterdam_(2018_TV_series) "New Amsterdam (2018 TV series)")*.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British actress (born 1985)

For other people named Janet Montgomery, see [Janet Montgomery (disambiguation)](/wiki/Janet_Montgomery_(disambiguation) "Janet Montgomery (disambiguation)").

| Janet Montgomery | |
| --- | --- |
| Montgomery at the 2015 [WonderCon](/wiki/WonderCon "WonderCon") | |
| Born | Janet Ruth Montgomery   (1985-10-29) 29 October 1985 (age 40)  [Bournemouth](/wiki/Bournemouth "Bournemouth"), [Dorset](/wiki/Dorset "Dorset"), United Kingdom |
| Occupation | Actress |
| Years active | 2008–present |
| Spouse | Joe Fox  ​ (m. 2019)​ |
| Children | 2 |

**Janet Ruth Montgomery** (born 29 October 1985) is an English film and TV actress. She first gained attention for her role as Ames in the second season of the television series *[Human Target](/wiki/Human_Target_(2010_TV_series) "Human Target (2010 TV series)")* (2010–11), and also for her appearance in the 2009 [straight-to-DVD](/wiki/Straight-to-DVD "Straight-to-DVD") film *[The Hills Run Red](/wiki/The_Hills_Run_Red_(2009_film) "The Hills Run Red (2009 film)")*. She played the lead character in the television drama *[Made in Jersey](/wiki/Made_in_Jersey "Made in Jersey")* (2012), cancelled after two episodes aired. From 2014 to 2017, she starred as the lead character, Mary Sibley, in the series *[Salem](/wiki/Salem_(TV_series) "Salem (TV series)")*. From 2018 to 2023, Montgomery played the role of Lauren Bloom on the [medical drama](/wiki/Medical_drama "Medical drama") television series *[New Amsterdam](/wiki/New_Amsterdam_(2018_TV_series) "New Amsterdam (2018 TV series)")*.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Janet_Montgomery&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Although trained as a dancer at the [Stella Mann College of Performing Arts](/wiki/Stella_Mann_College_of_Performing_Arts "Stella Mann College of Performing Arts"), [Bedford](/wiki/Bedford "Bedford"), Montgomery chose to pursue acting rather than dance professionally. Her lack of drama school training made it difficult for her to obtain acting representation initially. She achieved her breakthrough after producing a play with her friend [Gethin Anthony](/wiki/Gethin_Anthony "Gethin Anthony").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

In 2008, Montgomery was cast as the love interest of the character [Tony Stonem](/wiki/Tony_Stonem "Tony Stonem") in an episode of the teenage drama *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(UK_TV_series) "Skins (UK TV series)")*. She also appeared in both the TV movie *[Dis/Connected](/wiki/Dis/Connected "Dis/Connected")* and the short film *Flushed*.

The following year, Montgomery appeared in two [straight-to-DVD](/wiki/Straight-to-DVD "Straight-to-DVD") horror-genre releases filmed in [Sofia](/wiki/Sofia "Sofia"), [Bulgaria](/wiki/Bulgaria "Bulgaria") – *[The Hills Run Red](/wiki/The_Hills_Run_Red_(2009_film) "The Hills Run Red (2009 film)")* and the finale of the original *[Wrong Turn](/wiki/Wrong_Turn_(film_series) "Wrong Turn (film series)")* trilogy, *[Wrong Turn 3: Left for Dead](/wiki/Wrong_Turn_3:_Left_for_Dead "Wrong Turn 3: Left for Dead")* and played the role of Fallyn Werner, a teenage killer in the [Lifetime](/wiki/Lifetime_(TV_network) "Lifetime (TV network)") film *Accused at 17*.

In 2010, Montgomery appeared in her first theatrically released film, playing Giselle in the independent horror film *[Dead Cert](/wiki/Dead_Cert_(2010_film) "Dead Cert (2010 film)")* and the supporting role of Madeline in the [Academy Award](/wiki/Academy_Award "Academy Award")-nominated psychological thriller *[Black Swan](/wiki/Black_Swan_(film) "Black Swan (film)")* – playing a Little Swan to [Natalie Portman's](/wiki/Natalie_Portman "Natalie Portman") Oscar-winning portrayal of the Black Swan.

That same year Montgomery also signed on to play [Paul Rudd's](/wiki/Paul_Rudd "Paul Rudd") love interest in *[Our Idiot Brother](/wiki/Our_Idiot_Brother "Our Idiot Brother")*. After seeing her audition tape in Los Angeles, producers worked around her schedule on *[Entourage](/wiki/Entourage_(American_TV_series) "Entourage (American TV series)")* and flew her to New York to begin shooting. While working on *Entourage*, Montgomery was invited to appear on *[The League](/wiki/The_League "The League")*, a critically acclaimed [semi-improvised](/wiki/Improvisation "Improvisation") show about [American football](/wiki/American_football "American football"), and played Ambrosia the stripper in the first episode of the second season.

In December 2011, Montgomery starred as Princess Mithian, a love interest of [King Arthur](/wiki/King_Arthur "King Arthur"), in Series 4 Episode 11 of *[Merlin](/wiki/Merlin_(2008_TV_series) "Merlin (2008 TV series)")*,[[2]](#cite_note-2) later returning in Series 5 Episode 4 (alongside [James Fox](/wiki/James_Fox "James Fox")).[[3]](#cite_note-3)

In 2012, Montgomery played the lead role in the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") television series *[Made in Jersey](/wiki/Made_in_Jersey "Made in Jersey")*. The series was cancelled after two episodes aired. Two additional episodes aired on 22 December 2012.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Montgomery attracted the attention of [Stephen Poliakoff](/wiki/Stephen_Poliakoff "Stephen Poliakoff") via an audition tape she sent to casting director Andy Pryor for the role of Sarah in *[Dancing on the Edge](/wiki/Dancing_on_the_Edge_(TV_series) "Dancing on the Edge (TV series)")*.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] The five-part miniseries aired 4 February 2013 on [BBC Two](/wiki/BBC_Two "BBC Two").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

She also played opposite [David Tennant](/wiki/David_Tennant "David Tennant") in [BBC Four's](/wiki/BBC_Four "BBC Four") *[Spies of Warsaw](/wiki/Spies_of_Warsaw_(TV_series) "Spies of Warsaw (TV series)")*. She played Grace Van Helsing, "the prodigal daughter who returns to run the family newspaper (and presumably stake a few vampires)"[[4]](#cite_note-Hibberd-4) in the unsold [ABC](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company") pilot *Gothica*.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) Actor [Chris Egan](/wiki/Chris_Egan_(actor) "Chris Egan (actor)") played her love interest, the character [Dorian Gray](/wiki/The_Picture_of_Dorian_Gray "The Picture of Dorian Gray").[[4]](#cite_note-Hibberd-4)

From 2014 to 2017, Montgomery played the lead role of powerful witch Mary Sibley on the [WGN America](/wiki/WGN_America "WGN America") gothic horror series *[Salem](/wiki/Salem_(TV_series) "Salem (TV series)")*.[[7]](#cite_note-7) In October 2016, Montgomery started in a recurring role on the NBC drama *[This Is Us](/wiki/This_Is_Us_(TV_series) "This Is Us (TV series)")*.[[8]](#cite_note-This_Is_Us-8)[[9]](#cite_note-This_Is_Us_2-9) Also in 2016, Montgomery was cast in the lead female role in the comedy film *[Amateur Night](/wiki/Amateur_Night_(2016_film) "Amateur Night (2016 film)")* alongside [Jason Biggs](/wiki/Jason_Biggs "Jason Biggs").[[10]](#cite_note-driveshesaid-10)

In February 2018, Montgomery was cast in a main role in the NBC medical drama *[New Amsterdam](/wiki/New_Amsterdam_(2018_TV_series) "New Amsterdam (2018 TV series)")*, as Lauren Bloom, the head ER physician.[[11]](#cite_note-11) In October 2019, she played the role of Marguerite in the American drama thriller film [*Nighthawks*](/wiki/Nighthawks_(2019_film) "Nighthawks (2019 film)").

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Janet_Montgomery&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Montgomery was born in [Bournemouth](/wiki/Bournemouth "Bournemouth"), England. Her uncle was bass player Mike "Monty" Montgomery of [Zoot Money's Big Roll Band](/wiki/Zoot_Money%27s_Big_Roll_Band "Zoot Money's Big Roll Band").[[12]](#cite_note-12) She has a younger brother, Jason, who is also an actor.[[13]](#cite_note-13) In November 2018, Montgomery said that she was expecting her first child with boyfriend Joe Fox, a daughter, who was born in 2019.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) The couple married in Jamaica on 29 November 2019.[[16]](#cite_note-16) In June 2025, Montgomery announced that she is pregnant with their second child.[[17]](#cite_note-17) In December 2025, Montgomery confirmed that her and her husband welcomed their second child, a son.[[18]](#cite_note-18)
